7 months ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/23737415/comments/27453797)

I listened to each and everyone of these podcasts multiple times for several months when I would go on my walks; and I hate to admit it, but I could not focus and stay on point with the entire "Book of Genesis". It was so helpful. Once my mind stopped ruminating and chasing the latest new shiny object, and I had some structure in place, doing the entire "Book of Genesis" was so much easier/better, and these podcasts where the "cherry" on top all along. LOL. Thank you FB. :)

![Richard Sleigh](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/0c39a39b29ae739cf55953dca8414e63?d=mm&s=70)

Richard Sleigh

Awaiting Review 

2 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/23737415/comments/22830652)

I tried to skip ahead - wrote a polaris star, main goal, exchange value... and sent it to you. Now I realize that I really need to follow TMS in the order you put it together. The work I did was not a waste - the main goal I came up with will serve me well - and give me a focus for developing my habits and discipline. Then when I get to the drill down method by doing everything that comes before it... I should be ready + I need to find someone who will do this with me... So I'm listening to this set of podcasts over and over and checking the boxes as I complete stuff... thanks for putting this lifetime project together!

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/23737415/comments/15961571)

I came here because of the podcasts - I honestly thought that The Mystery School would be the podcasts in longer form; I'm pleasantly surprised as to the mix of formats and happy to see how you integrate the podcasts here: as a sort of summary and best-of (presumably) at the end of each Book Of... I can't wait to get to this part of The Book of Transformation to have that experience again.

Alisha Kabir

Awaiting Review 

5 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/23737415/comments/10615303)

rly resonate w episode 14 - i don't get the concept of burnout or overcreating in photography/digital art. the more I create, the more i create on top of that because the more i create the more inspired i get, and then i put out more work and then get back more from the outside world. I see it as an exponential feedback loop, not a reserve that depletes

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

When you're doing it right, that's exactly how it's supposed to feel. Perpetual motion. Finding and keeping that momentum is everything.
